title: National Rail Shutdown
description: Rail workers threaten a nationwide strike over safety standards and overtime rules.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: A rail shutdown would cost billions per day. The last major strike threat nearly froze the economy.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Which is why arbitration is necessary. We can’t let critical infrastructure be held hostage.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Arbitration is slow and risks prolonging uncertainty.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Giving in immediately encourages future strikes. Stability requires firmness.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Firmness is pointless if the economy collapses before negotiations finish.
